name: Río Segura Trail (GR 127)
name_original: Ruta por el cauce del Río Segura — circular
description: Jog the moderate 6.1-mile Río Segura Trail (GR 127) for riverside views, historical bridges, and gentle gradients in about 1 hour.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Alcantarilla are primarily shaped by the Segura River and the surrounding fertile plain, known as the "Huerta." This region features extensive green spaces, including parks and citrus orchards, providing a varied landscape for running. The terrain is generally flat with gentle gradients, making it accessible for different fitness levels. The town's commitment to developing integrated green areas further enhances the outdoor experience. Best jogging routes around Alcantarilla The most popular jogging route is Alcantarilla to Murcia via the Río Segura, an 11.5 miles (18.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 53 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers scenic views along the river. Another top favourite among local runners is Puente de las Ovejas, La Contraparada y Ermita Nuestra Señora de la Salud — circular, a moderate 5.5 miles (8.8 km) path. This circular route explores riverside areas and local landmarks. Local runners also love the Río Segura Trail (GR 127), a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 0 minutes. Jogging around Alcantarilla is defined by riverside paths, fertile plains, and integrated green spaces. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to longer river trails. The routes in Alcantarilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 700 runners have used komoot to explore Alcantarilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Alcantarilla?
A: Komoot offers nearly 40 diverse running routes around Alcantarilla. These range from easy loops through green spaces to more challenging trails along the Segura River, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, Alcantarilla offers several easy jogging routes, with 4 specifically categorized as easy. Many of the town's integrated parks and green spaces provide accessible, flat paths perfect for beginners or a relaxed run. You can also find 28 moderate routes that are suitable for those looking to gradually increase their distance.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging trails in Alcantarilla?
A: The terrain in Alcantarilla is generally flat, characterized by gentle gradients. You'll find routes along the Segura River, through fertile plains, and within the town's green spaces and citrus orchards. This provides a scenic and varied backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Alcantarilla?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1341927246/puente-de-las-ovejas-la-contraparada-y-ermita-nuestra-senora-de-la-salud-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de las Ovejas, La Contraparada y Ermita Nuestra Señora de la Salud — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.5-mile (8.8 km) path that explores riverside areas and local landmarks.

Q: What are some longer distance running options around Alcantarilla?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1341368060/alcantarilla-to-murcia-via-the-rio-segura" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcantarilla to Murcia via the Río Segura&lt;/a&gt; route is a difficult 11.5-mile (18.4 km) trail offering scenic views along the river. Another challenging option is a 10.1-mile (16.2 km) segment of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346037717/rio-segura-trail-gr-127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Segura Trail (GR 127)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Alcantarilla?
A: The running routes in Alcantarilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 30 reviews. Runners often praise the scenic riverside paths, the peacefulness of the fertile plains, and the well-integrated green spaces that make for an enjoyable outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or sights to see along the running routes?
A: Yes, many routes follow the Segura River, offering views of historical bridges and the natural beauty of the 'Huerta' agricultural landscape. While running, you might pass by areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3152197" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River&lt;/a&gt;. For more significant landmarks, the nearby city of Murcia offers attractions like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2925673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Murcia Cathedral&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3833926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Real Casino of Murcia&lt;/a&gt;, which can be reached via longer routes like the one connecting Alcantarilla to Murcia.

Q: Are the jogging routes in Alcantarilla suitable for families?
A: Many of Alcantarilla's integrated parks and green spaces, such as Plaza Campoamor and Plaza Félix Rodríguez de la Fuente, offer pleasant, accessible paths that are ideal for family-friendly jogs or walks. The generally flat terrain along the river also makes certain sections suitable for families.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Alcantarilla?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, many of the natural and green spaces in Alcantarilla, particularly along the Segura River and in the 'Huerta' areas, are generally dog-friendly. It's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What is the 'Witches' Trail' and where can I find it?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1342492525/witches-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Witches' Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 3.2-mile (5.1 km) running route in Alcantarilla. It offers a unique local experience, though its specific features are best discovered by exploring the trail itself.

Q: Are there opportunities for wildlife spotting while running in Alcantarilla?
A: Running along the Segura River and through the surrounding fertile plains and orchards can offer opportunities for wildlife spotting, particularly various bird species that inhabit the riparian and agricultural environments. The natural surroundings provide a serene setting for observing local flora and fauna.

Q: Is there public transport access to the jogging trails in Alcantarilla?
A: Alcantarilla has developed an integrated network of green spaces and paths, many of which are easily accessible from within the town itself. For routes further afield, local bus services within Alcantarilla and connecting to nearby areas in Murcia can provide access points, though specific trailheads may require a short walk from bus stops.
tips: A small, cozy, and very well decorated hermitage. They usually open it between April and May.
The Segura River plays a vital role in the local environment and has had a considerable impact on the history and development of the city.

The GR 127 trail follows the course of the Segura River throughout the region of Murcia, from the Cenajo Reservoir, on the provincial border with Castilla-La Mancha, to Orihuela, already in the Valencian Community.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: The specific circular route highlighted on komoot, which is about 9.8 kilometers long, typically takes around 1 hour to jog at a moderate pace. However, the full GR 127 is a long-distance trail, so overall duration varies greatly depending on which section you choose to explore.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The Río Segura Trail (GR 127) generally follows the Segura River, offering a diverse experience. Many sections feature gentle gradients and are mostly accessible, with some paved surfaces ideal for jogging. You'll encounter fertile plains, riverside vegetation, and areas with historical bridges. Further upriver, especially near its source, the terrain can become more rugged with volcanic areas, ravines, and steeper paths, sometimes including waterfalls and tunnels.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Many sections of the GR 127 are considered easy to moderate and are suitable for all skill levels, including beginners, especially those following the river with gentle slopes. For example, routes like Murcia to Orihuela are noted for their very low difficulty. However, the trail's extensive length means difficulty varies; some stages, particularly in mountainous areas or near the river's source, can be quite demanding and are rated as 'Hard hike' or 'Hard run'.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can experience a rich blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. Highlights include the picturesque rice fields of Calasparra, the impressive Cañón de Almadenes, and the historic Ricote Valley. You might also pass by the Hermitage of Our Lady of Health and enjoy a View of the Segura River. The trail also reveals ancient waterwheels, distinctive irrigation systems, and sometimes even thermal pools, castles, and archaeological sites.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de Santiago del Azahar, the Camino del Apóstol, and the Sendero de las Brujas. It also shares parts with the Vía Amable 1: Murcia - Alcantarilla, Transmurciana 20, and Transmurciana 21.

faqs.question: Is the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor trails in Spain are generally dog-friendly, specific regulations can vary by municipality or protected area along the extensive GR 127. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in populated areas, near wildlife, or on agricultural land, and to carry waste bags. Check local signage for any specific restrictions in the section you plan to visit.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required to access the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) itself, as it is a public long-distance path. However, if you plan to visit specific natural parks, cultural sites, or attractions directly adjacent to the trail, those individual locations might have their own entry requirements or fees.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) largely depends on the specific section and your tolerance for heat. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for outdoor activities in the Murcia region. Summers can be very hot, especially in the lower river sections, while winters are mild but can be cooler in the mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Río Segura Trail (GR 127)?
faqs.answer: Given the extensive length of the GR 127, parking options vary widely depending on your chosen starting point. Many towns and villages along the river, such as Murcia, Orihuela, or those near the Cañón de Almadenes, offer public parking facilities. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to research the particular section you plan to jog to find the most convenient parking areas.

faqs.question: Are there any specific sections of the GR 127 that are particularly good for jogging?
faqs.answer: For a relaxed jogging experience, sections with 'very low difficulty' like the route from Murcia to Orihuela are ideal, often featuring accessible and mostly paved surfaces. If you're looking for a more challenging run, some routes like 'Alcantarilla to Murcia via the Río Segura' are classified as 'Hard run' due to elevation changes, even on paved surfaces. The choice depends on your fitness level and preference for terrain.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or places to get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: As the Río Segura Trail (GR 127) passes through numerous towns and villages along its extensive course, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops to purchase refreshments. However, on more remote or rural sections, especially between settlements, services may be scarce. It's always wise to carry sufficient water and snacks, particularly for longer stretches.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Health
